name: Thread Synchronization (Locks)
description: Locks enforce mutual exclusion over shared data: a thread identifies the critical section and acquires a mutex, blocking or spinning if it is held; inside the section it performs read-modify-write safely, using atomics or memory barriers where needed, then releases the lock and wakes any waiting threads.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Threads share data"]
    N2["Find critical section"]
    N3["Acquire lock / mutex"]
    N4{"Lock available?"}
    N5["Block / spin wait"]
    N6["Enter critical section"]
    N7["Read-modify-write"]
    N8{"Race possible?"}
    N9["Atomic / barrier"]
    N10["Release lock"]
    N11["Wake waiting threads"]
    N12["Mutual exclusion"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 -->|no| N5
    N5 -->|retry| N3
    N4 -->|yes| N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|yes| N9
    N9 --> N10
    N8 -->|no| N10
    N10 --> N11
    N11 --> N12

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff

sources.title: Solution of a problem in concurrent programming control
A new solution of Dijkstra's concurrent programming problem
